lesson.menu_desc: Reading a customer health report + comparatives and trends -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Presenting Customer Health & Metrics
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita | Aula 16 -- Presenting Customer Health & Metrics

paras: This report gives a quick summary of your account's health for the third quarter. The overall health score is seventy-eight out of one hundred. Compared to last quarter, when the score was seventy-two, this is a clear increase, and the trend over the year is positive. A health score is a single number that combines how much your team uses the product, how happy your users are, and how many support issues you open. A higher score means a healthier account.

Three metrics improved this quarter. First, your adoption rate increased from sixty percent to seventy-four percent, which is higher than our target of seventy. In other words, more of your users now log in and work in the platform every week. Second, retention is strong: almost all of your licenses are still active, so very few users left. Third, churn decreased again. Churn is the share of users who stop using the product, and a lower number here is better. Compared to the same quarter last year, churn is much lower.

One area needs attention. Last month, the number of support tickets rose quickly — a significant rise, not a small one — and daily logins in the finance team decreased a little. We believe the new dashboard confused a few users, so the next steps are simple. First, we will run a short walkthrough for the finance team. After that, we will track the same metrics every week and compare them to the target. Our goal is for every key number to move in the right direction before the next review.
reading.source: Adapted for class use from a customer success health report

gist.prompt: What is the best one-line summary of this report?
choices: It is a price list for new product licenses and support plans.
It summarises the account's health for the quarter: most metrics improved, but support tickets and finance logins need attention.
It explains, step by step, how to install the new finance dashboard.

items: The overall health score is higher this quarter than last quarter.
The score is seventy-eight, compared to seventy-two last quarter — a clear increase.
A lower churn number is worse for the account.
Churn is the share of users who stop using the product, so a lower number is better.
The adoption rate is below the target of seventy percent.
The adoption rate increased to seventy-four percent, which is higher than the target of seventy.
Support tickets rose significantly last month.
The report calls it a significant rise, not a small one.
In your own accounts, which single metric tells you the most about customer health, and why?
The report is honest about a problem (the support tickets). How do you present bad numbers to a customer without losing their trust?
If churn had increased instead of decreased, what would your next steps be?

listenings.text: Hi Patricia, this is Raj from the data team, sending you a quick voice note before the quarterly review with David. I just finished the customer health report, so let me walk you through the headline numbers. The overall health score is seventy-eight, which is higher than last quarter's seventy-two, so the trend is clearly positive. Adoption is the big win. The adoption rate increased from sixty percent to seventy-four percent, well above our target of seventy. Retention also looks strong, and churn decreased again, so we are now below the target for the third quarter in a row. There is one area to watch, though. Support tickets rose quite a lot last month, a significant rise, not a slight one, and daily logins in the finance team dropped a little. My read is that the new feature confused a few users, so I would be honest about that in the review and show the plan to fix it. Compared to where we were a year ago, this account is in much better shape. Good luck with David, and call me if you want to change any of the slides.

Hi Patricia, this is Karen, the executive sponsor on our side, leaving you a short message after today's review. I really appreciated how clear the health report was. For the first time, I could actually see the trend, instead of just a wall of numbers. My team was very happy to hear that our adoption rate is higher than the target and that churn is lower than last quarter. Those two metrics matter the most to my leadership. I was also glad that you were honest about the support tickets. The rise looked big on the slide, but your explanation made sense, and the plan to fix it gave me confidence. One small request for next time. Could you add a simple line that compares each metric to our original target, so I can share it with my boss in one screenshot? Other than that, this was the most useful review we have had. The numbers are moving in the right direction, and your story around them was easy to follow. Thank you, and let us keep this format for the next quarter.
